title: Map Missingness
description: Records gaps, low resolution, redaction, or disputed coverage in a map dataset itself — distinct from schemas/landscape-memory.schema.json's erased_features, which records physical landscape features reported as destroyed or removed. A map can be missing data about a place that still physically exists, and a place can be erased while its map data remains accurate; this schema and landscape-memory track those two failure modes separately. First draft, not yet reviewed by Kemi.

examples.area_description: Coastal segment near a disputed settlement boundary
